title: Performance — Optimization
description: Add performance tooling to your file manager. Create a benchmark decorator that measures and prints execution time, and optimize the search function using compiled regex patterns and functools.lru_cache for repeated lookups.
test_code:
assert callable(benchmark), 'benchmark must be a callable decorator'
import functools
assert hasattr(compile_pattern, 'cache_info'), 'compile_pattern should use lru_cache (has cache_info)'
assert callable(compile_pattern), 'compile_pattern must be callable'
compiled = compile_pattern(r'\d+')
assert hasattr(compiled, 'search'), 'compile_pattern should return a compiled regex with search method'
assert hasattr(compiled, 'match'), 'compile_pattern should return a compiled regex with match method'
result = compiled.search('abc123')
assert result is not None, 'Compiled pattern should match digits in abc123'
assert result.group() == '123', f"Should match '123', got '{result.group()}'"
@benchmark
def sample_func(x):
    return x * 2
assert callable(sample_func), 'Decorated function should still be callable'
result = sample_func(5)
assert result == 10, f'Decorated function should return correct value, got {result}'
assert sample_func.__name__ == 'sample_func', f'Decorator should preserve function name via functools.wraps, got {sample_func.__name__}'
cache_info = compile_pattern.cache_info()
assert cache_info.hits >= 0, 'cache_info should be accessible'
assert 'functools' in sys.modules, 'functools module should be imported'
assert 'time' in sys.modules, 'time module should be imported'
acceptance_criteria: benchmark decorator measures and prints execution time
benchmark preserves the wrapped function's name via functools.wraps
benchmark returns the original function's return value
compile_pattern returns a compiled regex object
time module is used for measuring execution time
hints: Use @functools.wraps(func) inside your decorator to preserve metadata
Measure time with start = time.time() ... elapsed = time.time() - start
The decorator pattern: def benchmark(func): @functools.wraps(func) def wrapper(*args, **kwargs): ...
compile_pattern just needs to return re.compile(pattern)
lru_cache is already applied via the decorator — just implement the function body
